reasoning: Query asks for hanabi (fireworks) festivals but get_festivals returns 312 general intangible cultural property festivals with zero fireworks entries in the visible results. The tool returns real data but fails to filter or rank for the specific fireworks sub-category the user needs.
missing_data: Fireworks-specific festivals (Sumida River, Nagaoka, PL Fireworks etc.); date information is null for almost all items; no season/timing metadata.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword or category filter to get_festivals so queries like 'hanabi' surface fireworks events. Alternatively surface get_spots or search_area with 花火大会 before generic festival listing.

reasoning: Same problem as L3-01 but in Japanese. 312 festival records returned with no fireworks relevance. The lang switch to ja works correctly (name/description in Japanese), but the content mismatch is identical.
missing_data: 花火大会 specific entries; dates; locations with fireworks schedules.
improvement_hint: Implement keyword-based sub-category filtering or a dedicated fireworks event layer. A search_area({'q':'花火大会'}) call would likely outperform get_festivals here.

reasoning: Snow festivals query on Hokkaido returns count:0 for local festivals. The national_heritage fallback lists UNESCO items like Dainichido Bugaku (Akita) and Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama) — none related to Hokkaido snow festivals. The Sapporo Snow Festival is the obvious answer but is entirely absent.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, Sounkyo Ice Festival; any Hokkaido-specific winter event data.
wrong_data: National heritage fallback items are geographically and thematically irrelevant to Hokkaido snow festivals.
improvement_hint: Index Hokkaido snow festivals as a distinct data source; ensure prefecture filter on national_heritage fallback also applies; add seasonal tagging.

reasoning: Query asks for rural/countryside life experience (田舎暮らし体験). get_japan_heritage returns 104 Japan Heritage stories starting with ancient Asuka and Kamakura, which are historical/cultural sites rather than rural living experience destinations. The data is real and well-structured but does not address the intent.
missing_data: Satoyama experiences, farmstay accommodation (農泊), rural community experience programs, Shirakawa-go or similar living-history rural areas.
improvement_hint: Add a search_area call with '農泊' or '里山' before falling back to heritage browsing; add theme-based filtering to get_japan_heritage for rural/satoyama themes.

reasoning: search_area({'q':'紅葉'}) returns 81 results with named parks, shrines, heritage-designated locations including 紅葉谷公園 (Hiroshima, prominence 0.75), 桜井神社 (Osaka, National Treasure), and others with coordinates and heritage labels. Response is grounded, diverse, and serviceable for autumn foliage planning though mostly name-matches rather than famous koyo spots.
missing_data: Famous koyo spots like Nikko, Arashiyama, Korankei are not visible in truncated results; season/peak timing data absent.
improvement_hint: Add a season metadata field; consider boosting known koyo destinations in ranking; add description_ko translations for Korean users.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet, non-touristy meditation temples. search_area({'q':'修行'}) returns a Tokyo temple (Shugyo-ji), a Fukushima 修行院, and noise from Yamagata DMO pages and municipal site cookie notices with no meditation content. 曼殊院 (Kyoto, heritage) appears but lacks description for accessibility. The 'non-touristy' constraint is impossible to enforce from this data.
missing_data: Zazen experience temples (Eiheiji, Sojiji), nojuku/meditation retreat info, 'shukubo' (temple lodging) data, accessibility and booking details.
improvement_hint: Add a dedicated 'experience' or 'activity' category for temple stays; filter out cookie-consent-only DMO noise; surface shukubo listings.

reasoning: User asks about northern lights (aurora borealis) in Japan — a niche phenomenon visible only in Hokkaido during strong solar events. Response returns Sanrio Puroland (Tokyo), cookie-notice-only DMO pages, an Iwate tourism features page, and Okinawa botanical garden. No aurora-relevant content. The query is niche but real aurora sightings in Japan do occur.
missing_data: Hokkaido aurora viewing spots (Abashiri, Rishiri, Wakkanai), solar activity advisories, any reference to rare aurora sightings in northern Japan.
wrong_data: Sanrio Puroland and Okinawa garden are completely irrelevant.
improvement_hint: Add an aurora/astronomical events data source; or return a graceful 'limited data' response rather than unrelated noise; improve relevance ranking to penalize zero-overlap results.

reasoning: Query asks for 100-year-old houses (古民家 stays) in Japan. get_hotels(Gifu) returns Gifu hotel listings (correct prefectural scope given Gifu has many machiya areas), but all results are generic: Airbnb apartment, hostel, standard hotels. No kominka or heritage accommodation flagging. The corpus has no way to distinguish traditional old farmhouse lodging from standard hotels.
missing_data: Kominka-specific lodging tags; Shirakawa-go gassho-zukuri stays; age/heritage status of accommodation; Hida Folk Village area ryokan listings.
improvement_hint: Add lodging type metadata including 'kominka', 'machiya', 'gassho-zukuri'; scrape specialized platforms or MLIT kominka lodging registry; tag heritage accommodation distinctly.

reasoning: Thai-language query for off-the-beaten-path cherry blossom spots. search_area({'q':'桜'}) returns 164 results but top hits are Sakurada-mon gate (Tokyo landmark), Sakuragi Station (railway station), 桜井神社 (shrine), and 桜島 (Sakurajima volcano) — name-matched on 桜 but not actual cherry blossom viewing spots. Famous hidden spots are buried or absent. Lang='th' means Thai translations missing for most results.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions; actual cherry blossom spots (not name-matches); lesser-known sakura spots like Takato, Miharu Takizakura.
improvement_hint: Distinguish '桜の名所' (sakura spots) from entities merely named after sakura; add a bloom-season tag; improve Thai translation coverage.

reasoning: Photography/dramatic landscapes query. search_area({'q':'絶景'}) returns 51 results. Top hits include a photo download page (nav-chrome), Yamagata tourism in Chinese/Traditional Chinese, a Niigata 絶景 page, and solid wikidata entries like 鬼ヶ城 (Mie, scenic beauty + natural monument) and 象潟 (Akita, scenic). Wikidata entries are useful but buried below noisy DMO pages.
missing_data: Photography-specific access info, golden hour timing, tripod/drone restrictions; iconic spots like Furukawa tunnel, Biei patchwork, etc.
improvement_hint: Rank wikidata scenic beauty designated attractions above generic DMO pages; add photography-relevant attributes (accessibility by car, timing, restrictions).

reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor hot springs (not hotel baths). search_area({'q':'露天風呂'}) returns 72 results including Iwate onsen listings with genuine outdoor bath descriptions, Yamaguchi outdoor bath article, Hōsenbo Onsen (Ehime, detailed description with outdoor baths), and Iwate mountain onsen spots. Content is relevant and grounded. The 'not in hotels' constraint can't be fully enforced but some results suggest genuine outdoor settings. Arabic translation absent for most.
missing_data: Arabic-language content; clarity on which baths are hotel-only vs. public/wild; famous rotenburo like Takaragawa, Sennin-buro.
improvement_hint: Add Arabic translation coverage; tag baths by type (public, private, roadside, outdoor-only); surface Hokkaido/Tohoku wild onsen prominently.

reasoning: User wants to swim with wild dolphins. search_area({'q':'イルカ'}) returns Kyoto culture ambassador page (completely irrelevant), Noto Island aquarium (captive dolphin show, opposite of wild), Niigata seafood market, and fishing market listings. Mikurajima (Tokyo) and Amitori Bay (Amami) are the known wild dolphin swim spots but do not appear.
missing_data: Mikurajima dolphin swimming (most famous wild dolphin spot in Japan), Amami wild dolphin encounters, any marine wildlife encounter experiences.
wrong_data: Kyoto culture ambassador page has no dolphin content — pure noise.
improvement_hint: Add marine wildlife activity data; filter municipal_scrape results that have zero semantic overlap with the query entity; consider dedicated 'wildlife experiences' taxonomy.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional architecture untouched by modernization — 重伝建 (Judenken) districts. get_japan_heritage returns 104 Japan Heritage stories starting with Asuka period history and Kamakura culture — not traditional streetscape preservation districts. Judenken districts (Gion, Shirakawa-go, Tsumago, etc.) are the canonical answer but this tool returns narrative heritage stories.
missing_data: Judenken preservation districts (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区); machiya streetscapes; Edo-period townscapes; Gion, Tsumago, Magome, Uchiko data.
improvement_hint: Add a dedicated Judenken dataset or filter; get_japan_heritage could expose architecture-themed stories; search_area({'q':'重伝建'}) would likely perform better for this query.

reasoning: Excellent response. Vietnamese query for Ainu culture in Hokkaido. search_area({'q':'アイヌ'}) returns 92 results with highly relevant content: アイヌ伝統景観 (Important Cultural Landscape, Hokkaido), アイヌ民族博物館, 国立アイヌ民族博物館 (Upopoy, Shiraoi), 二風谷アイヌ文化博物館, 北海道立アイヌ総合センター, 川村カ子トアイヌ記念館, 弟子屈コタン. All in Hokkaido with coordinates. Heritage designations present. Directly answers the intent.
missing_data: Vietnamese-language descriptions; booking/access info for Upopoy; cultural experience programs beyond museums.
improvement_hint: Add Vietnamese translation for name/description fields; add 'experience' tags for institutions offering hands-on Ainu cultural activities.

reasoning: Outstanding match. Query for cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Top results: 臼杵磨崖仏 (Oita, prominence 0.9, National Treasure + Special Historic Site), 大谷磨崖仏 (Tochigi, prominence 0.9, Special Historic Site), 清水磨崖仏 (Kagoshima), 白山道奥磨崖仏 (Kanagawa), 大岩弘法院磨崖仏 (Fukuoka). Precise, geographically diverse, with coordinates and full heritage designation labels. Directly answers the niche query.

wrong_data: 蘭渓道隆賛達磨図 is a painting, not a magaibutsu — slight category pollution in tail.
improvement_hint: Filter out movable art objects (paintings, scrolls) when querying for architectural/landscape heritage; add access information.

reasoning: Query asks for volcano hikes doable in a single day from Tokyo. search_area({'q':'火山'}) returns 'Hiyama' mountain in Fukuoka (no coords), then multiple Kumamoto/Aso volcano DMO pages in Chinese. improvement_hint: Add geographic proximity filtering; tag volcanoes with distance-from-major-hub metadata; surface 富士山 and 伊豆大島 when Tokyo day-trip + volcano is queried.

reasoning: Indonesian query to eat freshly made tofu at a temple (精進料理/temple cuisine context). search_area({'q':'豆腐'}) returns Fukui tourism site (language selector only), Chinese-text ryokan in Iwate, Kumamoto gourmet pages in Chinese about ramen/horse meat, and one useful GI item: 岩出山凍り豆腐 (freeze-dried tofu GI product from Miyagi). reasoning: Query for fishing villages 'frozen in time'. search_area({'q':'漁港'}) returns Niigata seafood market (lively, not frozen in time), Kitakyushu sea page (copyright only), then Kesennuma DMO page with good content about its living fishing port culture, and 野付湾 (Hokkaido, Ramsar wetland). 漁村夕照図 is a painting in Tokyo (noise). The Kesennuma entry is genuinely useful for atmospheric fishing port content.

reasoning: Query for traditional Japanese papermaking (washi) workshops. get_local_specialty(category='craft') returns 231 METI-designated traditional crafts with high-quality data. Top entries are Nibutani Attus (Ainu textile), Oitama Tsumugi, Ugo Shina Cloth — all crafts but not washi. The corpus almost certainly contains washi entries later in the list. Tool call is correct but without 'washi' keyword filter, relevant items are buried.
missing_data: Echizen Washi (越前和紙), Mino Washi (美濃和紙), Tosa Washi — all METI-designated; experience/workshop booking info; artisan contact details.
improvement_hint: Add keyword/name filter parameter to get_local_specialty so 'washi' or '和紙' can narrow 231 results to relevant ones; or use search_area({'q':'和紙'}) as a complementary call.

reasoning: Query for jidaigeki (period drama) scenery locations. search_area({'q':'宿場'}) returns excellent content: Nagano post-town article (Suharashuku/Nojiroshuku on Nakasendo), Ueda Yanagimachi post-town description with detailed atmosphere, 旧名手宿本陣 (Wakayama, Important Cultural Property + Historic Site), 宿根木 (Sado, Important Preservation District), and Tokaido post-town cycling article. Very relevant and actionable for the intent.
missing_data: Coordinates for most wikidata entries missing; Magome, Tsumago (most iconic) not explicitly visible in truncation.

reasoning: Query for non-crowded beaches. search_area({'q':'ビーチ'}) returns 81 results with wikidata beach entities: 南知多ビーチランド (amusement park, not a beach), 大磯ロングビーチ (water park), 波の上ビーチ (Okinawa, prominence 0.75), 砂山ビーチ (Miyako, Okinawa), several Okinawa beaches, 米原ビーチ (Ishigaki). Results are real beaches but skew toward known spots; the 'not crowded' constraint cannot be addressed from this data.
missing_data: Crowd/popularity data to distinguish hidden vs. popular beaches; Sanriku coast beaches, Oga Peninsula, Tohoku/Hokkaido beaches that are genuinely uncrowded.

improvement_hint: Tag beach entities by type (natural vs. artificial/water park); add popularity/crowd estimates; surface lesser-known prefectures for 'uncrowded' queries.

reasoning: Query for small, old-school izakaya alley drinking with locals. search_area({'q':'横丁'}) returns 79 results but top hits are: 横丁公園 (park, Tokyo), Saga tourism in Chinese (no yokocho content), はい!からっと横丁 (Yamaguchi, irrelevant), 瀞八丁 (scenic gorge, completely off-topic), then multiple National Treasure art objects in Kyoto with '横' in their name. reasoning: German query for rural rail travel in Japan. search_area({'q':'ローカル線'}) returns 58 results with excellent content: Nagano local train art trip article (Bessho Line detail, timetable, scenery), Ibaraki nemophila trip by local rail, Amami access page, and a DMO plan chunk about Tsugaru local railways with actual statistics. Content is substantive and directly relevant to the intent.

reasoning: Firefly viewing query. search_area({'q':'ホタル'}) returns 72 results with strong hits: ホタルの里 park (Hyogo), Firefly Museum of Toyota Town (Yamaguchi), hotaru viewing page (Kitakyushu), Yamaguchi hotaru-bune (boat firefly viewing, June 5-21, price ¥2000/¥1000, national monument designation), Nagasaki firefly guide with optimal viewing time (8-9pm, humid nights) and etiquette. Very practical and actionable content.
missing_data: Specific coordinates for viewing spots; nationwide coverage beyond west Japan; peak season calendar.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates to firefly viewing spots; include national natural monument firefly habitats (Genji-botaru sites); add season/month metadata.

reasoning: Russian query for winter crane (鶴=tsuru) watching spots. search_area({'q':'鶴'}) returns 161 results but top hits are 鶴岡八幡宮 (Kamakura shrine, prominence 0.95), 鶴林寺 (Hyogo temple, National Treasure), 鶴舞公園 (Nagoya park) — all named with the kanji 鶴 but not crane habitat. Izumi city (Kagoshima), where 10,000+ hooded cranes winter, and Kushiro (red-crowned cranes) are absent from visible results.
missing_data: Izumi Crane Observatory (Kagoshima, world's largest crane wintering ground), Kushiro Tsurui Crane Sanctuary (Hokkaido, red-crowned crane), seasonal winter timing.
improvement_hint: Separate wildlife/ecological queries from cultural entity name-matching; add a wildlife sanctuary or natural habitat data layer; distinguish 鶴 as a bird from 鶴 as part of place names in ranking.
